scope: No-auth future data source registry for documented public machine-readable entry points.
rules: Backend-pullable via ordinary HTTP requests or public object storage access.
Do not add feature logic in this registry.

sources.title: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program GeoJSON feeds
sources.tier_reason: Already implemented in repo as the baseline environmental event connector.
sources.first_output: Already implemented.
data_caveats: Magnitude does not prove impact.
Coverage is source-limited and event-specific.
rate_limit_notes: Use bounded polling and fixture-first tests.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Already in repo.
do_not_do: Do not duplicate connector work.
sources.verification_notes: Already implemented in repo and documented in existing earthquake docs.

sources.title: NASA EONET events API
sources.tier_reason: Already implemented in repo as the second environmental event source.
sources.first_output: Already implemented.
data_caveats: Events can have multiple geometries over time.
Source event context does not prove impact or final footprint.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Already in repo.
do_not_do: Do not duplicate connector work.
sources.verification_notes: Already implemented in repo and documented in existing EONET docs.

sources.title: USGS Ashcam API
sources.tier_reason: Already present in webcam subsystem as the current no-auth federal webcam source.
sources.update_cadence: provider-driven current webcam inventory and frames
sources.first_output: Already implemented.
data_caveats: Viewer usefulness depends on frame freshness and camera status.
Camera imagery is context, not proof of ash extent.
legal_compliance_notes: Preserve USGS attribution and source-specific camera terms.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Already in repo.
do_not_do: Do not duplicate connector work.
sources.verification_notes: Already documented in webcam subsystem and present in code.

sources.title: USGS Volcano Hazards Program API
sources.owner: U.S. Geological Survey Volcano Hazards Program
sources.tier_reason: Official no-auth API with direct GeoJSON/status outputs and strong environmental plus aerospace value.
sources.first_output: Volcano status records and elevated-volcano GeoJSON.
data_caveats: Coverage is U.S. volcanoes, not a global volcano feed.
Status is advisory context and not a plume or ash-dispersion model.
legal_compliance_notes: Do not overstate aviation hazard precision.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Normalize elevated volcano GeoJSON and advisory status fields.
do_not_do: Do not claim ash dispersion or flight-route impact beyond source status.
Do not invent global coverage.
sources.verification_notes: Docs page and GeoJSON endpoint returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: Tsunami.gov Atom and CAP feeds
sources.tier_reason: Official no-auth Atom and CAP feeds with strong coastal hazard value and simple polling model.
sources.first_output: Alert records with issued time, center, bulletin type, and bulletin URL.
data_caveats: Bulletin semantics differ between informational and warning products.
Feed geometry may require careful interpretation.
legal_compliance_notes: Do not present feed ingestion as sole coastal situational awareness.
rate_limit_notes: Event-driven feed polling should remain conservative.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Atom feed polling with CAP link preservation and alert deduplication.
do_not_do: Do not infer impact area beyond source messaging.
Do not collapse informational statements into warnings.
sources.verification_notes: Homepage exposed Atom and CAP links; PAAQ and PHEB feed URLs returned XML without auth.

sources.title: FAA NAS airport status XML
sources.tier_reason: Public FAA XML endpoint is directly useful to aerospace workflows and returned machine-readable content without auth.
excluded_endpoints: FAA key-based developer APIs outside this no-auth registry
sources.update_cadence: updates roughly every minute per FAA NAS Status app guidance
sources.first_output: Airport delay and closure records linked to canonical airport ids.
data_caveats: XML shape is FAA-specific and may require custom parsing.
DTD reference in payload should not be treated as authoritative schema validation for first slice.
legal_compliance_notes: Do not claim full NAS operational awareness beyond endpoint contents.
rate_limit_notes: Use bounded polling and avoid aggressive refresh.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Parse active airport events and normalize airport closures, ground stops, and delay categories.
do_not_do: Do not build a broad FAA scraper around the NAS web UI.
Do not assume all airport status semantics are globally comparable.
sources.verification_notes: Public XML endpoint returned 200 and machine-readable content from a plain HTTP request.

sources.title: NOAA Aviation Weather Center Data API
sources.tier_reason: Official documented API with JSON and GeoJSON outputs, high aerospace value, and explicit rate guidance.
sources.update_cadence: product-specific, often minute-scale for core observations
sources.first_output: Airport and route weather context records.
data_caveats: AWC has product-specific limits and recent API changes.
Forecast and advisory products are contextual and not proof of operational outcome.
rate_limit_notes: Most endpoints should not be consumed more frequently than 1 request per minute per thread.
Large pulls should prefer cache files.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Normalize METAR plus one advisory family for selected airports.
do_not_do: Do not poll large unbounded result sets.
Do not assume browser CORS behavior equals backend permissions.
sources.verification_notes: Docs and sample METAR endpoint returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NOAA CO-OPS Tides and Currents API
sources.tier_reason: Official public JSON API with strong marine and coastal utility and low integration cost.
sources.first_output: Station observations and predictions near the marine viewport.
data_caveats: Station coverage is coastal and station-based, not wall-to-wall coverage.
Prediction products and observed products should stay distinct.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Water level observations for nearby stations with station metadata and freshness fields.
do_not_do: Do not mix predictions and observations without labeling them separately.
sources.verification_notes: Docs and sample datagetter JSON request returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NOAA NDBC realtime buoy files
sources.tier_reason: Official no-auth flat files are easy to fetch and useful for marine and coastal context.
sources.first_output: Latest buoy observation records by station.
data_caveats: Realtime files are automated-QC operational data, not final archive products.
Data type availability varies by station.
rate_limit_notes: Limit retrieval frequency to a practical cadence.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Ingest nearest-station standard met and wave summary files with station metadata.
do_not_do: Do not assume every station has every file type.
sources.verification_notes: Official FAQ documents HTTPS file access; sample realtime file returned publicly.

sources.title: USGS Water Services Instantaneous Values
sources.tier_reason: Official no-auth JSON endpoint with clear hydrology value and low integration cost.
sources.first_output: Gauge observations by site with streamflow and stage fields.
data_caveats: Coverage is station-based and parameter-based.
Observed gauge data should remain distinct from forecast hydrology.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Normalize streamflow and gauge-height observations for a bounded site set.
do_not_do: Do not present point gauges as full flood extent.
sources.verification_notes: Docs page and sample IV JSON endpoint returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NOAA SWPC public JSON products
sources.tier_reason: Official no-auth JSON product feeds with immediate aerospace context value.
sources.first_output: Space-weather alert and index records.
data_caveats: Global indices are contextual, not target-specific operational proof.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Normalize current SWPC alerts and K-index summaries.
do_not_do: Do not overclaim mission impact without domain-specific correlation.
sources.verification_notes: Official products directory and sample JSON alert feed returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NOAA National Hurricane Center GIS and RSS feeds
sources.tier_reason: Official GIS RSS feeds are public and valuable for marine, coastal, and environmental context.
sources.first_output: Active advisory summaries and linked GIS products by basin.
data_caveats: Feed items link to downstream GIS products whose semantics differ by product type.
Storm products are advisory context and should preserve issuance windows.
sources.recommended_first_slice: RSS/XML advisory record ingestion before shapefile or raster handling.
do_not_do: Do not pretend RSS summaries are full storm geometry.
sources.verification_notes: Official GIS RSS docs and sample XML feed returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NOAA Storm Prediction Center RSS products
sources.tier_reason: Official RSS family verified and useful for contextual severe-weather products.
sources.first_output: Severe weather product records with valid windows and source links.
data_caveats: RSS gives product records and links; polygon or map geometry may require separate handling.
Products are forecast/advisory context.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Normalize SPC product metadata from RSS and preserve outbound source links.
do_not_do: Do not collapse forecast products into observed event claims.
sources.verification_notes: General, watch, mesoscale, convective outlook, and mesoscale bulletin RSS URLs returned XML without auth.

sources.title: NOAA SPC preliminary storm reports CSV
sources.tier_reason: Official CSV is easy to ingest and strong observed severe-weather context for the platform.
sources.first_output: Observed storm report records with preliminary flag and report type.
data_caveats: Reports are preliminary and not final climatology.
Location precision varies by report.
rate_limit_notes: Daily or bounded refresh is sufficient for first slice.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Normalize today and yesterday reports with report type and caveat text.
do_not_do: Do not present preliminary reports as final verified event catalogs.
sources.verification_notes: Sample `today.csv` returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: OpenFEMA disaster declarations API
sources.tier_reason: Official read-only API with no key requirement and direct machine-readable disaster declaration records.
sources.first_output: Disaster declaration records by state, county, and FIPS.
data_caveats: This is administrative declaration context, not real-time event detection.
Entity shape and paging should be honored.
sources.recommended_first_slice: DisasterDeclarationsSummaries with state, county, incident type, and dates.
do_not_do: Do not present declarations as immediate operational ground truth.
sources.verification_notes: Sample API endpoint returned JSON publicly without auth; docs are official but one docs URL variant returned 403 to urllib.

sources.title: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
sources.tier_reason: Official JSON feed is public, easy to pull, and useful for future cyber/reference context.
sources.first_output: KEV records indexed by CVE and due date.
data_caveats: This is a cyber advisory catalog and should not be geographically mapped by default.
KEV inclusion does not by itself prove exploitation against a specific target.
rate_limit_notes: Catalog updates do not require aggressive polling.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Normalize CVE, vendor, product, due date, and notes.
do_not_do: Do not auto-map to geography or imply victim attribution.
sources.verification_notes: Official catalog page and JSON feed returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NEXRAD Level II public object storage
sources.tier_reason: Public and well-documented, but binary radar handling is high complexity and should start at file metadata only.
sources.first_output: Radar-site and object-availability metadata, not rendered radar imagery.
data_caveats: Level II files are binary and large.
Rendering or interpretation is out of scope for a first slice.
legal_compliance_notes: Preserve NOAA attribution and open-data notices.
rate_limit_notes: Object listing should be bounded by site and time prefix.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Site-level file availability metadata for nearest radar context.
do_not_do: Do not attempt full radar decode/render in the first connector.
sources.verification_notes: Registry docs are public; S3 bucket listing returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NOAA MRMS public products
sources.tier_reason: Public and valuable, but product family and binary formats make first-slice scope control important.
sources.first_output: MRMS product metadata index and selected contextual overlay candidates.
data_caveats: Product family is extensive and binary-heavy.
First slice should focus on discovery metadata, not raw raster rendering.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Expose product-name, latest-file, and freshness metadata for a small MRMS subset.
do_not_do: Do not scrape the MRMS viewer.
Do not treat research-heavy products as trivial overlays.
sources.verification_notes: Public directory index and sample gzipped MRMS file returned without auth.

sources.title: NOAA GOES GLM public object storage
sources.owner: NOAA GOES-R Series / NOAA Open Data Dissemination
sources.tier_reason: Public object storage is confirmed, but NetCDF-style product handling should start with prefix metadata and summaries.
sources.first_output: Recent GLM object availability and metadata summaries by time prefix.
data_caveats: GLM products are not a simple point feed.
Interpretation should preserve that the source is satellite-derived lightning context.
legal_compliance_notes: Preserve NOAA attribution and open-data notices.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Prefix-level metadata discovery and freshness checks before any lightning summarization.
do_not_do: Do not promise finished lightning density mapping in the first connector.
sources.verification_notes: GOES public bucket listings returned XML without auth; official registry docs are public.

sources.title: NOAA IOOS HF Radar ERDDAP
sources.tier_reason: Public ERDDAP access is confirmed, but dataset selection and current-vector normalization need a narrow first slice.
sources.first_output: Dataset discovery metadata for current-vector feeds near target coastlines.
data_caveats: ERDDAP exposes many datasets; first slice should choose a small stable subset.
Surface current vectors require careful normalization and confidence caveats.
rate_limit_notes: Use search or metadata endpoints first.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Dataset search and metadata discovery for one coastal region before gridded pulls.
do_not_do: Do not attempt full national current-vector ingestion first.
sources.verification_notes: ERDDAP index and search JSON endpoints returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NWS CloudGIS warning and advisory services
sources.tier_reason: Official public OGC services are available, but first use should be overlay-first rather than normalized evidence records.
sources.first_output: Overlay service metadata and bounded warning polygon queries.
data_caveats: Service family is broad and product semantics vary.
Overlay availability should not be mistaken for a unified event model.
rate_limit_notes: Start with bounded capabilities and small queries.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Capabilities check plus one small warning-layer query for overlay use.
do_not_do: Do not normalize every warning product into the same evidence schema without careful typing.
sources.verification_notes: Official CloudGIS docs and sample WFS/WMS capabilities requests returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NIFC WFIGS public wildfire perimeter service
sources.tier_reason: Public ArcGIS FeatureServer query is available, but wildfire perimeter semantics and lifecycle handling need a narrow first slice.
excluded_endpoints: Restricted NIFC or EGP login-only datasets
sources.first_output: Current perimeter metadata and bounded wildfire overlay queries.
data_caveats: Perimeter products require incident-state caveats and timestamp awareness.
Current perimeters are not a universal fire activity feed.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Current perimeter count and bounded incident overlay metadata.
do_not_do: Do not mix in restricted wildfire datasets.
Do not claim completeness beyond the current public perimeter layer.
sources.verification_notes: Sample ArcGIS FeatureServer count query returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: GDACS public disaster feeds
sources.owner: Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System
sources.tier_reason: Public interagency feeds are easy to poll, but they should remain contextual and not replace source-specific hazard connectors.
sources.update_cadence: feed updates approximately every 6 minutes per feed reference
sources.first_output: Cross-disaster alert context records.
data_caveats: GDACS explicitly describes information as indicative and not sole decision support.
Use as context, not as a replacement for source-specific hazard feeds.
legal_compliance_notes: Preserve GDACS attribution and disclaimer language.
sources.recommended_first_slice: General all-events RSS ingestion with source-specific category tagging.
do_not_do: Do not present GDACS alone as proof or final ground truth.
sources.verification_notes: Official feed reference page and RSS feed returned publicly without auth.

sources.title: NOAA National Water Prediction Service API
sources.tier_reason: Official docs are public, but the exact low-risk first endpoint should be pinned from the API docs before coding.
sources.first_output: Gauge-level NWPS metadata and forecast/observation summaries after endpoint pinning.
data_caveats: Forecast and observation products must remain distinct.
The first endpoint should be selected from the official API docs before implementation.
rate_limit_notes: Follow API docs once the exact endpoint is chosen.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Pin one documented gauge/forecast endpoint from NWPS docs and build a fixture-first parser.
do_not_do: Do not guess undocumented endpoints from the web app.
sources.verification_notes: Official docs page is public, but the exact first implementation endpoint was not pinned during this pass.

sources.title: NOAA nowCOAST OGC and REST services
sources.tier_reason: Official service family is documented, but direct service probes returned 403 from a plain HTTP client in this environment.
sources.first_output: Service-capability and health checks only until backend-safe access is confirmed.
data_caveats: Service family is large and overlay-oriented.
Backend-safe plain HTTP access should be re-verified before coding.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Service health and capabilities verification rather than feature integration.
do_not_do: Do not assume the browser app implies stable backend access.
sources.verification_notes: Official nowCOAST site is public, but direct REST service probes returned 403 from urllib during this pass.

sources.title: Minnesota 511 public machine endpoint candidate
sources.tier_reason: Public site exists, but a stable public machine endpoint was not cleanly verified and the web app exposes reCAPTCHA.
excluded_endpoints: Account, alert, or reCAPTCHA-gated web flows
sources.first_output: Verify whether a public ArcGIS or documented no-auth data endpoint exists.
data_caveats: Public web app behavior does not equal a stable backend API.
Site includes reCAPTCHA on the web app path.
legal_compliance_notes: Do not scrape account or protected flows.
rate_limit_notes: Unclear until a public endpoint is verified.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Only a source-verification pass, not implementation.
do_not_do: Do not scrape the interactive map.
Do not bypass reCAPTCHA or account flows.
sources.verification_notes: Public site is reachable, but no stable no-auth machine endpoint was pinned during this pass.

sources.title: GDELT public APIs
sources.tier_reason: Public and documented, but it is context-heavy media data rather than a primary operational source.
sources.first_output: Future context-only article summaries.
data_caveats: Media-derived context is not operational ground truth.
Geocoding and entity matching require strong caveats.
legal_compliance_notes: Follow documented API usage and attribution.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Only future context summarization, not core event ingestion.
do_not_do: Do not treat GDELT as authoritative event truth.
sources.verification_notes: Public documented API exists; source is deferred due fit and interpretation caveats.

sources.title: Wikimedia EventStreams recentchange
sources.tier_reason: Public and machine-readable, but not a primary source for the platform's current hazard and operations goals.
sources.first_output: Future context stream ingestion only if specifically assigned.
data_caveats: General-purpose edit stream is not domain-specific evidence.
rate_limit_notes: Streaming ingestion should be isolated if ever used.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Deferred unless a future context task explicitly needs it.
do_not_do: Do not treat edits as vetted facts.
sources.verification_notes: Sample SSE stream returned publicly without auth; source remains deferred by fit.

sources.title: SANS ISC / DShield public API
sources.tier_reason: Public API entry exists, but this is a non-government cyber-context source and not a first-priority platform input.
sources.first_output: Future cyber-context summaries only.
data_caveats: Use only as context and preserve source framing.
legal_compliance_notes: Review source-specific usage expectations before any implementation.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Deferred until explicitly assigned.
do_not_do: Do not treat as authoritative victim attribution.
sources.verification_notes: Public API landing page returned without auth; source is deferred by scope and governance fit.

sources.title: abuse.ch URLhaus public JSON feed
sources.tier_reason: Public JSON feed works, but this remains a future cyber-context candidate rather than a current platform priority.
sources.first_output: Future cyber-context feed ingestion only if explicitly assigned.
data_caveats: Threat-intel feeds require careful scope and attribution handling.
legal_compliance_notes: Review abuse.ch feed terms before implementation.
sources.recommended_first_slice: Deferred until explicitly assigned.
do_not_do: Do not map or attribute victim geography by default.
sources.verification_notes: Public JSON feed returned without auth; source is deferred by scope and governance fit.

sources.title: MarineTraffic scraping
sources.tier_reason: Scraping-based path violates registry standards and carries clear compliance risk.
sources.first_output: Do not use.
data_caveats: Rejected due scraping/compliance risk.
sources.recommended_first_slice: None.
do_not_do: Do not scrape MarineTraffic.
sources.verification_notes: Explicit avoid entry per project rules.

sources.title: VesselFinder scraping
sources.tier_reason: Scraping-based path violates registry standards and carries clear compliance risk.
sources.first_output: Do not use.
data_caveats: Rejected due scraping/compliance risk.
sources.recommended_first_slice: None.
do_not_do: Do not scrape VesselFinder.
sources.verification_notes: Explicit avoid entry per project rules.

sources.title: OpenPhish or PhishTank without source-specific vetting
sources.tier_reason: Not approved until a specific no-auth machine endpoint and usage posture are individually verified.
sources.first_output: Do not use without separate verification.
data_caveats: Source and access posture must be verified individually.
legal_compliance_notes: Do not assume these feeds satisfy no-auth production requirements.
sources.recommended_first_slice: None.
do_not_do: Do not treat generic public lists as approved production feeds.
sources.verification_notes: Explicit avoid entry per project rules.

sources.title: Generic all-state DOT or 511 integration
sources.tier_reason: Every DOT or 511 source must be vetted individually; a generic omnibus integration is unsafe and misleading.
sources.first_output: Do not use as a blanket source family.
data_caveats: Access terms and machine endpoints vary by state.
legal_compliance_notes: Vetting must occur source by source.
sources.recommended_first_slice: None.
do_not_do: Do not claim that one pattern covers all DOT or 511 sources.
sources.verification_notes: Explicit avoid entry per project rules.

sources.title: Viewer-only web apps without stable public data endpoints
sources.tier_reason: Viewer-only apps are not acceptable backend connectors when no stable public machine endpoint is documented.
sources.first_output: Do not use.
data_caveats: HTML view availability is not an API contract.
sources.recommended_first_slice: None.
do_not_do: Do not build connectors from undocumented HTML viewers.
sources.verification_notes: Explicit avoid entry per project rules.

sources.title: Sources requiring login, key, email, signup, request access, or CAPTCHA
sources.tier_reason: Outside the no-auth registry contract by definition.
sources.first_output: Do not use in this registry.
data_caveats: Fails the core no-auth rule set.
sources.recommended_first_slice: None.
do_not_do: Do not add such sources to production no-auth integrations.
sources.verification_notes: Explicit avoid entry per project rules.

sources.title: Restricted wildfire datasets requiring login or special access
sources.tier_reason: Public NIFC open data should not be mixed with restricted wildfire datasets in the no-auth registry.
sources.first_output: Do not use.
data_caveats: Outside no-auth scope.
legal_compliance_notes: Do not mix public and restricted wildfire feeds under one connector.
sources.recommended_first_slice: None.
do_not_do: Do not use restricted wildfire datasets in this registry.
sources.verification_notes: Explicit avoid entry per project rules.

sources.title: Utility outage maps without verified public machine endpoints
sources.tier_reason: Do not use until a specific stable machine-readable public endpoint is verified source by source.
sources.first_output: Do not use.
data_caveats: Many outage maps are viewer-only or operationally brittle.
sources.recommended_first_slice: None.
do_not_do: Do not scrape outage maps.
sources.verification_notes: Explicit avoid entry per project rules.
